senses.definition: complete and total,  with no imperfections or exceptions

examples.x: The painting depicted the landscape with absolute realism, capturing every tiny detail.
The lab procedures demanded absolute cleanliness to avoid contaminating the samples.
Figure skaters strive for an absolute flawless technical execution without a single missed step.
By surgically repairing the damage, the doctors were able to restore her vision to an absolute 20/20.

senses.definition: (of measurements, information, etc.) free from errors and matching facts

examples.x: The scientist presented an accurate report based on years of research.
His description of the event was accurate, matching all the details from the news.
The data was checked and found to be accurate, with no discrepancies.
The historian‚Äôs account of the war was accurate, drawing from primary sources.

senses.definition: existing in reality rather than being theoretical or imaginary

examples.x: Despite the rumors, the actual cost of the project was within the budget.
She compared the advertised features of the product with its actual performance.
He found the actual location of the meeting after some confusion.
Her explanation didn‚Äôt match the actual events.

senses.definition: added or extra to what is already present or available

examples.x: They needed to purchase additional supplies to complete the project.
The company hired additional staff to handle the increased workload.
She provided additional examples to clarify the concept for the students.
The hotel offered additional amenities, such as a gym and spa, for guests.
He requested additional time to review the contract before signing.

senses.definition: done, provided, or arranged before a future event or expected time

examples.x: The team provided advance notice of the upcoming changes to the schedule.
She made an advance payment to secure her reservation for the vacation.
He received advance notice about the meeting to prepare adequately.

senses.definition: easy to see or notice

examples.x: His apparent discomfort was evident from his body language.
His apparent confusion indicated that he didn't understand the instructions.
The apparent beauty of the landscape took her breath away.
The apparent cause of the fire was faulty wiring in the building.
The apparent damage to the car suggested it had been in an accident.
It became apparent that they had no intention of finishing the project on time.

senses.definition: suitable or acceptable for a given situation or purpose

examples.x: Wearing casual attire is appropriate for a picnic.
Using formal language is appropriate in a business meeting.
Bringing a gift is appropriate when attending a party.
Offering condolences is appropriate when someone experiences a loss.
Using safety gear is appropriate when working with machinery.
The company provided appropriate resources for new employees.

senses.definition: not able to see

examples.x: The blind man navigates the city using a white cane and a guide dog.
She learned Braille to read books since she is blind.
The blind musician plays the piano by memory, relying on touch and sound.
The blind athlete competes in track and field events with the assistance of a guide.
The blind student uses screen reading software to access digital content.

senses.definition: short in duration

examples.x: The meeting was brief, lasting only ten minutes.
He gave a brief explanation of the concept.
The phone call was brief, lasting only a few minutes.
She made a brief appearance at the party.
The storm brought a brief period of heavy rain.

senses.definition: having a large distance between one side and another

examples.x: His shoulders were broad, giving him a powerful and imposing appearance.
The bridge spanned a broad river, allowing for the passage of large ships underneath.
The road widened into a broad highway as we approached the city.
The broad leaves of the tree provided ample shade on hot summer days.
The garden bed was 3 meters broad, providing ample space for a variety of plants.
The table was 4 feet broad, making it perfect for large family dinners.
The river was half a mile broad at its widest point.

senses.definition: having the required quality or ability for doing something

examples.x: She is a capable leader, able to guide her team through challenges with confidence.
His capable hands crafted a beautiful piece of furniture from raw materials.
The capable student excels in academics, consistently achieving high grades.
The capable athlete demonstrates exceptional skill and determination on the field.
The capable doctor provides compassionate care and accurate diagnoses to her patients.

senses.definition: serving to identify or distinguish something or someone

examples.x: His characteristic wit and humor always brightened the mood in the room.
It‚Äôs hard to ignore the characteristic smell of coffee in the morning, especially in this caf√©.
The bird's characteristic song could be heard every morning at dawn.
The artist's paintings were known for their characteristic use of bold colors and abstract shapes.
The way she reacts to challenges is a characteristic trait of her personality.

senses.definition: having the highest importance

examples.x: The chief concern of the community is addressing unemployment among young people.
He is the leader, but his chief responsibility is overseeing operations.
She took on the chief role in managing the project, which was a big challenge.
Environmental concerns are chief in the discussions, despite other economic factors.
In this project, the chief objective is to develop sustainable solutions for environmental conservation.

senses.definition: involving many different parts or elements that make something difficult to understand or deal with

examples.x: The legal case was complicated, involving numerous laws and regulations.
Her feelings for him were complicated, influenced by a mix of emotions.
The algorithm used in the program was complicated, with multiple nested functions.
Understanding the economic situation was complicated, as it involved various global factors.
Explaining the scientific theory to the students was complicated, as it required breaking down complex concepts.
The instructions for the project were too complicated to follow.

senses.definition: feeling worried or troubled about a particular situation or issue

examples.x: She felt concerned about her son's performance in school and decided to talk to his teacher.
He felt concerned about the impact of climate change on future generations.
The concerned look on his face indicated his worry about the upcoming deadline.
The concerned parents waited anxiously for news about their missing child.
He seemed concerned about the budget cuts and their effect on the company's future.

senses.definition: not clear or easily understood

examples.x: The new tax laws are so confusing that even experts are having trouble deciphering them.
His explanation of the scientific theory was confusing, leaving the students with more questions than answers.
The teacher's explanation of the math problem was so confusing that most of the class couldn't follow along.
The user interface of the app was confusing, making it difficult for users to navigate.
The confusing directions led us in the wrong direction.

senses.definition: having awareness of one's surroundings

examples.x: The detective remained conscious of the subtle clues in the crime scene, piecing together the evidence.
The lifeguard stayed conscious of the swimmers in the pool, ready to respond to any emergencies.
The hiker remained conscious of the changing weather conditions as he continued along the trail.
Even in her sleep-deprived state, she remained conscious of the time, knowing she had a deadline to meet.
She was conscious of the people around her as she walked through the busy city streets.

senses.definition: happening continuously without stopping for a long time

examples.x: The constant noise from the construction site made it difficult to concentrate.
Her constant dedication to her studies paid off when she graduated with top honors.
The constant rain in the region led to flooding in low-lying areas.
He showed constant kindness to everyone he met, earning him the respect of his peers.
The constant changing of regulations made it challenging for businesses to adapt.

senses.definition: involving a large company

examples.x: The corporate headquarters of the company are located in New York City.
She attended a corporate event to network with other professionals in her field.
The corporate culture emphasizes teamwork and innovation.
They developed a corporate strategy to expand into international markets.
Corporate taxes play a significant role in government revenue collection.

senses.definition: having great importance, often having a significant impact on the outcome of a situation

examples.x: Time management is crucial for meeting deadlines.
Proper planning is crucial for the success of any project.
Following safety protocols is crucial in high-risk environments.
Feedback from customers is crucial for improving product quality.
Good communication skills are crucial in building strong relationships.

senses.definition: done on purpose

examples.x: His deliberate choice of words conveyed a sense of seriousness.
The decision to delay the project was a deliberate choice to ensure quality.
Her deliberate actions showed careful consideration of the consequences.
She made a deliberate effort to include everyone in the discussion.

senses.definition: including many specific elements or pieces of information

examples.x: The report provided a detailed analysis of the company's financial performance.
The map had detailed markings showing all the hiking trails in the national park.
Her instructions for assembling the furniture were very detailed, with step-by-step illustrations.
The detective took detailed notes during the investigation to ensure nothing was overlooked.
The artist's painting was incredibly detailed, with intricate brushstrokes capturing every nuance.

senses.definition: facing or pointing toward a lower level or position

examples.x: She felt the downward pull of gravity as she descended the staircase.
The downward slope of the hill made hiking difficult.
The downward trajectory of the ball indicated a drop in speed.
The downward direction of the arrow on the sign pointed to the basement.
The downward sweep of the waterfall created a misty veil.

senses.definition: (of a system or machine) achieving maximum productivity without wasting much time, effort, or money

examples.x: The efficient assembly line increased productivity while reducing production costs.
Using public transportation is more efficient than driving a car, especially in congested areas.
The company's efficient use of resources maximized profits and minimized waste.
The new software streamlined processes, making the office more efficient and organized.
An efficient irrigation system conserves water while ensuring crops receive adequate moisture.

senses.definition: involving or describing the whole of something

examples.x: She spent the entire day cleaning the house, leaving no corner untouched.
He read the entire book in one sitting, captivated by the story from beginning to end.
The entire population of the town gathered for the annual festival, filling the streets with laughter and music.
The entire class worked together to solve the math problem, pooling their knowledge and skills.
She ate the entire cake by herself, savoring each delicious bite.

senses.definition: according to moral duty and obligations

examples.x: It is important for businesses to make ethical decisions that consider the well-being of all stakeholders.
The journalist followed ethical guidelines in reporting the sensitive story.
Ethical considerations are crucial when conducting medical research involving human subjects.
Teaching children about ethical behavior helps them develop strong moral values.
The company's ethical stance on environmental sustainability is reflected in its policies and practices.

senses.definition: uniform in dimensions or quantity

examples.x: The survey showed an even distribution of opinions among the participants.
The rows of plants were spaced at even distances in the garden.
The pie was cut into even slices so everyone got the same amount.

senses.definition: using or having the power to decide on important matters, plans, etc. or to implement them

examples.x: The executive order was issued by the president to enforce the new policy.
Her executive role involved overseeing the day-to-day operations of the company.
Executive decisions are made by top-level management to guide the direction of the organization.
The executive team meets regularly to review performance and set objectives for the organization.

senses.definition: remarkable or very unusual, often in a positive way

examples.x: The chef's culinary skills were extraordinary, creating dishes that delighted the palate.
The artist's extraordinary talent allowed them to create breathtaking works of art.
The singer's voice was extraordinary, captivating audiences with its beauty and power.
The athlete's performance in the championship game was truly extraordinary, leading their team to victory.
The scientist made an extraordinary discovery that revolutionized the field of medicine.

senses.definition: capable of bending easily without breaking

examples.x: The flexible plastic tubing is used to transport liquids in laboratories and industries.
The rubber hose is flexible, allowing it to bend around corners without kinking.
The flexible silicone spatula easily bends to scrape every last bit of batter from the bowl.
Rubber bands are flexible and can stretch to hold together stacks of papers or other objects.

senses.definition: designed in a way that can be folded or bent so it takes up less space
referring to the first of two things mentioned
used to express one's disapproval of a name or term given to someone or something because one believes it is inappropriate

examples.x: The so-called experts on social media often lack credible credentials.
She introduced us to her so-called friend, who later betrayed her trust.
The so-called miracle cure turned out to be ineffective against the illness.
He dismissed the allegations as baseless and criticized the so-called evidence presented.
The so-called secret recipe for the famous dish was finally revealed to the public.

senses.definition: carefully arranged and in order

examples.x: She kept her desk neat and organized, with everything in its proper place.
The kitchen counters were wiped clean, leaving them neat and tidy.
The garden was meticulously maintained, with neat rows of flowers and vegetables.
She admired how neat and tidy her friend's room was, with everything in its proper place.
The teacher appreciated the students' neat work in their notebooks, with no messy scribbles or stray marks.

senses.definition: strange or unexpected in appearance, style, or behavior

examples.x: The bizarre sculpture in the park, with its surreal combination of animal and human features, intrigued passersby.
The bizarre behavior of the man, who insisted on wearing a chicken costume to work, raised eyebrows among his coworkers.
She had a bizarre dream last night, in which she was flying on a giant banana through a cotton candy sky.
The bizarre sight of a cow wandering through the city streets left residents scratching their heads.
His bizarre collection of vintage medical equipment, displayed prominently in his living room, made guests uneasy.

senses.definition: appearing to have a certain quality, yet lacking it in reality

examples.x: His apologies seemed superficial and insincere.
His superficial charm masked his true intentions.
She found his compliments to be superficial and insincere.
She realized that their friendship was only superficial and lacked genuine connection.

senses.definition: hidden or no longer visible to one
